Bankrupt four years later, Charles family endured poverty doggedly pursued venture: stabilization rubber commercial use.
1844, after establishing Naugatuck India-Rubber Company, Goodyear patented vulcanization process discovery that made possible range indispensible commercial products, from tires sneaker treads.
Despite this success, Goodyear battled patent infringements debt until death 1860.

This thesis examines work reformer Lugenia Burns Hope community organization, Neighborhood Union, case study unpack scholarly characterizations black elite uplift strategies during early 20th century.
Neighborhood Union established 1908 Atlanta Hope women from community build stronger neighborhoods combat deleterious effects 1906 Race Riots Crow laws.
Neighborhood Union settlement houses provided basic extracurricular services, including kindergartens working mothers, vocational classes, lecture series.
organization's exceptional, multi-class leadership structure enabled members black poor working classes lead their projects with assistance Neighborhood Union resources.
Hope's background provides evidence against broad generalizations black elite paternalistic, vision creating democratic communities that diminished class barriers provides counter narrative characterizations clubwomen black elite engaging respectability politics their social work.
Understood within historical sociopolitical context, Hope's life work also challenge mainstream narratives Progressive Social Gospel movement.
Pierson, Madeleine, Model Empowerment: Lugenia Burns Hope's Community Vision Through Neighborhood Union" (2016).
